mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in industrial production signal expanding manufacturing output, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month rank correlation between INDPRO change and future 60-day returns falls below 0.05 or the direction becomes negative for any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above a 4.8% threshold indicates deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.8%, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are positive more than 50% of the time over any 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm broad-based demand and supply expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue prospects and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month increase > 0.5% and INDPRO month-over-month increase > 0.5%
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction adds no predictive power over single-factor models (p > 0.10 in Wald test).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime defined by year-over-year CPI above 4%, any additional month-over-month price increase signals persistent overheating that compels hawkish policy expectations, depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In months where CPI YoY exceeds 4% and CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.2% MoM, 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive more than 40% of the time over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts rising above the 1,200 thousand unit level reflect strong residential investment and consumer confidence, boosting near-term economic sentiment and supporting positive S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1200, 5-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative more than 50% of the time over any 3-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment gains exceed 200,000 and the federal funds rate remains below 0.5%, the economy is in a low-rate, strong-job-growth sweet spot that amplifies corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint condition does not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or its information coefficient is negligible (< 0.03) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment below 4.5%), further declines in the unemployment rate signal increasing wage pressure and potential Fed tightening, which hurts equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE below 4.5%, dropping UNRATE leads to positive 60-day returns more than 50% of the time, or the negative predictive signal has rank IC below -0.03 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 1% on a sequential basis indicates accelerating economic activity that boosts corporate profits and investor confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 QoQ growth exceeds 1%, 60-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than 50% of the time over any 10-year walk-forward period.
